observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the costs of major repairs to their appropriate PPE accounts, to compute for their corresponding depreciation and to submit contracts and other supporting document relative to the disbursements.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the affected accounts to their correct balances.

Recommendation
3.6	We recommended that LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper classification and presentation distinguishing the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic in the preparation of financial position.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop using the current appropriation/allotment for payment of the prior year’s obligations/ transactions;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts all valid and legal expenditures at the end of the calendar year and shall be obligated properly as accounts payable; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Ensure the application of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan to be approved by the Local Chief Executive and submit it to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts and to prioritize the preparation of BRS for accounts with fewer transactions to ensure regular compliance with the requirement in the future; and
Direct the Local Chief Executive to augment the workforce under the Accounting Office, taking into consideration competency and qualification.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers for cash advances for Salaries, Wages, Honoraria, and similar payments are supported by approved payroll, and the amount to be granted should be based on the net amount of the payroll, and to be meticulous in verifying the completeness and validity of supporting documents attached to the DV and liquidations to ensure accurate and proper recording of transactions; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to explain the lack of signatures on the payroll to avoid the issuance of a notice of disallowance.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the contract loan and amortization schedule of each loan and ensure that prospective payments are complete with supporting documents.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the BAC Secretariat to submit all approved contracts together with their supporting papers and to ensure that all prospective contracts are submitted within 5 days upon its perfection; and
Revisit and identify the gaps in the BAC and to constitute a new member/committee, if warranted.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal HRM Officer to submit all Job Order Contracts; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require JOs to submit the required DTR and accomplishment report as attachments to the payrolls, duly verified by the official who has direct supervision of the JO.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims for traveling expenses are in accordance with EO No. 77; and
Direct the officials to refund the said overpayment of traveling expenses.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide the appropriate and necessary documentary requirements to substantiate the propriety and reasonableness of the claims; and
Instruct the GSO to ensure that meals, snacks, or goods to be purchased are itemized and not described in general terms and, to the extent practicable, offer sufficient information to justify the appropriateness of the price.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers not later than March 15, 2025; and
Require all Department Heads to attend seminars or training to deepen their knowledge of rules and regulations on government expenditures.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers; and
Consider hiring more personnel to augment the staff requirements of the Accounting Office.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit revised Obligation Requests for the specified expenditures and ensure that complete and accurate obligation requests accompany all disbursement vouchers in future periods.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Treasurer to consider opening a bank account for 20% DF in the same bank with that of the GF-NTA, if warranted; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the monthly transfer of 20% DF to its depository account.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Local School Board to furnish copies of the SEF budget and supplemental budgets, if any, to the Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office within the prescribed period; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the DepEd, copy furnished to the local Sanggunian concerned, Dep Ed and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

Recommendations

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit/transfer real property tax share of the Special Education Fund to its depository account regularly every month; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain SEF cashbooks for Cash in Treasury, Cash In Bank and Cash Advances.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the 2022 bank reconciliation statement for trust fund bank account and ensure that the reports will be submitted regularly; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the CY 2022 bank statements, subsidiary ledger and photocopy of the cash in bank cashbook.

We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct an actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the General Services Officer to be witness by any representative of the Commission On Audit.
The General Service Officer should maintain stock cards of each property, plant and equipment bearing property number while the Municipal Accountant should maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment.
Identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
The Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
The Municipal Administrator should update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.
Provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.
Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond in order that the property officer can apply for a bond.

We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer should stop the practice of using cash advance to pay the operating expenses of the Municipality.
Limit cash advances to the specific purpose of which the cash advance was drawn.
All disbursement should be issued with check direct to the name of the payees, concerned employees, suppliers and etc.
For procurement of goods and infrastructure, processing should be done on the procedures as mandated by Republic Act 9184.
Disbursement through reimbursement are strongly discouraged except travelling expenses.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive if possible, to facilitate the adoption of Payroll-thru-bank system, which is a much safer scheme that precludes hold-ups and similar incidents involving payrolls, and promotes operational efficiency.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer the following:
All accountable forms should be issued in strict numerical sequence to account the issuances of accountable forms in sequence and facilitate reconciliation.
Require the municipal accountant to review carefully the collections remitted by the Cashier to prevent the possible commission of irregularities thereby protecting the interest of the agency.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer the following:
To verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collector and check the same against new balances on hand column.  Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I.
The treasurer/designated officer accountable for receipts, stamps and other accountable forms in their possession or in the hands of deputies and other employees entrusted therewith shall keep adequate permanent record books and contain all the information as set forth in Sec. 95 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I.

We have recommended the following:
Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly with the regulation to ensure effective performance on the task assigned and submit monthly report of collections and report of accountable forms and turnover the used accountable forms. And used and issued accountable forms in sequence.
Exert effort to locate the unpresented accountable forms.
Submit an affidavit of loss of the accountable forms and properly notify all concerned parties of such loss by way of notice of loss and publication.

We have recommended the following to the Municipal Treasurer:
To remit/deposit intact daily all collections to the treasury of the agency to protect the funds of the government.
To diligently update their cashbooks daily and to maintain an Excel format of their cashbooks to minimize, if not avoid, errors on the total collections to be deposited.
Designate a Liquidating Officer in order to strictly monitor the collections and deposits of the Collecting Officers in order that the Municipal Treasurer to focus on the disbursements only.

We have recommended the following:
The concerned accountable offices to submit monthly the required reports and not to accumulate them on a lump sum basis so that from time to time, the audit team can update on the financial status of the Municipality.
We also require the national offices such as the DA, DILG, DSWD, DOLE and other offices to secure liquidation reports before transferring additional funds to the said LGU in order not to delay the implementation of their programs and projects and to avoid the possible misappropriation or losses or projects not implemented especially during transition period.

We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor to arrange a new mode of delivery of the bank statements to the Municipality either by pick-up by an authorized personnel or by registered mail.
The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality.
She should prepare necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income and stale checks to bring the cash in bank account in its correct balance.
She should prepare a letter informing the servicing bank of the wrong postings they made if any and secure copies of debit memos to validate those amounts debited by the bank without any corresponding entries in the books.
The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts of the Municipality should authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll online access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.
Give utmost priority and close supervision in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to ensure timely submission.
Direct management to augment the work force assigned in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.

We have recommended to:
Local Chief Executive to give emphasis on the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under the Local Government Code.
Management filling up the vacant position of municipal assessor in order to prepare and submit the estimated real property tax collectibles and updating the record on tax assessment.

We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Vice Mayor to apply for a fidelity bond, approved the disbursement vouchers for the appropriation of the sanggunian only and shall be allowed to countersigned in the check drawn.
Issue resolution authorizing the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer as the recognize signatory of their check disbursement for the Office of the Vice Mayor and Sanggunian Bayan.
The Municipal Budget Officer to compute the monthly appropriation of the office of the Vice Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan Members to be transferred to the Account of the general fund – vice mayor appropriation.

We have recommended the following:
All end-users of the LGU should to its Department Head the needed office supplies and materials.
Each department of the LGU should submit a Project Procurement Management Plan to the BAC Secretariat depending on the budget.
The BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMP of the different department for consolidation to the Annual Procurement Plan which will be the legal basis of the LGU procurement of goods, supplies and materials.

We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should train a regular and permanent employee as the Municipal Budget Officer.
Recommend her to attend training that involved budgeting.
The Municipal Budget Officer should maintain the required registries of funds / appropriations / allotments and obligations and utilization in order to avoid juggling of funds and for internal control purposes. Also, to facilitate the realization of the programs / projects / activities embodied in the Approved Annual Budget.
Strict adherence to the provision of laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended to the Human Resource Officer furnishing the Accounting office with a copy of Schedule of Earned Leave Credits for all LGU personnel with money value as basis by the Accounting Office to record the leave accruals. Meantime, that budget was not yet provided, just disclosed it in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor to create a GAD committee with the following composition:
CHAIR:	Local Chief Executive (LCE)
MEMBERS:
Heads of LGU departments and other offices relevant to Gender Mainstreaming;
Chairs of the following Sanggunian Committees:
Women, Children and Family
Appropriations
Representative/s from the following groups:
PNP Women’s Desk;
Indigenous Peoples (IPs) (where existent)
Persons with Disabilities (PWDs)
Women’s Organization or recognized NGO
Private Sector
Academe
President of the Liga ng mga Barangay;
Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) Federation President
b. 	We also have recommended to the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.
c. 	Further, incorporate the approved GAD plan/budget in the budget proposal for CY 2018 and onwards to ensure that the cost of implementing the GAD activities is part of the approved budget and that at least 5% of the total agency appropriations as authorized under the annual GAA, shall correspond to the activities supporting the GAD.
d. 	The Department Heads to identify the GAD related expenditures in their existing LGU Budget and Plan to be incorporated in the GAD Plan.

We have recommended to the Bids and Award Committee to comply the guidelines under RA 9184 to arrive at an efficient and effective project implementation. Correct all the deficiencies noted for adequate management operation for the interest of a good governance.

We have recommended the following:
Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to prepare monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund, certified correct by the Accountant.
The Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We have recommended the following:
The Local School Board to prepare a budget for Special Education Fund and make deliberations in accordance with the guidelines.
The proceeds shall be allocated for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development as determined and approved by the local school board.

We have recommended the following:
a.  The Municipal Accountant Designate should prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality.
b.   The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts of the Municipality to authorize the Municipal Accountant Designate to enroll in i-access or online banking of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.
c.   If possible, appoint the Municipal Accountant as prescribed by the Civil Service Law or in accordance with the qualification standard set by the CSC.
d.  Request the Local Chief Executive to augment additional personnel in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.
e. The Municipal Accountant Designate should prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, and stale checks to bring the cash in bank account in its correct balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the delay is primarily caused by the difficulty in securing a copy of bank statements.  The bank mails the statements at a much later date.  However, it is apparent that they only prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements at one time which should have provided an ample time for the preparation of the reports.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a. Revenue Collectors to prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer for review.
b. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Consolidated RAAF for submission to the Auditor’s Office through the Municipal Accountant’s Office.

We have recommended the following:
a. All end-users of the procuring entity should individually prepare and submit the PPMP to the BAC Secretariat.
b. Direct the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the Annual Procurement Plan based on all the individual APP/PPMPs of the end-users, copy furnished the local auditor.
c. BAC thru its secretariat to prepare and submit the Annual Procurement Plan as required pursuant to provision of Section 7 of Republic Act 9184 to achieve efficiency, effectiveness and economy in the procurement of supplies and materials. Ensure that this requirement is complied with and that all purchases of goods, supplies and materials are included in or covered by the approved Annual Procurement Plan.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive should establish the 5% calamity fund broken down into the 70% preparedness fund and the 30% should be allocated to the Quick Response Fund or stand-by fund for relief and recovery projects and activities.
b. The Municipal Treasurer should open a special trust fund account to transfer the unexpended LDRRMF at the end of the year.
c. Adhere strictly to Republic Act No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund of Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: Balanced already transferred to the Calamity Trust Fund Account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the regular conduct of the physical count and inventory reporting on PPE and Inventories by the General Services Office and Accounting Unit or a Committee that will be created for that purpose pursuant to Section 2 of PD 1445.

We required the accounting and General Services Offices to observe the procedures on the preparation, proper recording, and the updating of SL/Property Cards for PPE; and Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards for Inventories.

We recommended the management to strictly comply with the provision of Section 119 of PD 1445.  Refrain from using current appropriation/allotment for payment of prior year’s obligations/transactions.

We recommended the accounting unit to take up in the books of accounts all valid and legal expenditures that are not paid at the end of the calendar year shall be properly obligated as accounts payable in accordance with laws and regulations to preclude distortion of the current annual budget and possible misstatement of income.

Effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to rectify the error, as follows:

Prior Period Adjustment        P xxx
   Operating Expenses                            P xxx    

We also recommended the municipal accountant to inform all cost center heads in advance to submit supporting documents to the budget office for obligation of the expenses already incurred during the year to be charged to the current year appropriation.

We recommended the Municipality of Poona-Piagapo would require concerned government employees to stop the amount being given an to strictly observe existing laws and guidelines in granting such additional benefits to some of its employees.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer  to deposit the balance of the 30% of 5% Trust Fund account.

We also recommended the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Council (LDRRMC) to submit the following:

Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan  (LDRRMFIP) and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF or Annexes A and B, respectively of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2002

Copies of separate Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) – for Quick Response Fund, for Maintenance & Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.

Likewise, we recommended that the Management adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes.  Also the Municipal Accountant should analyze and determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances of some trust liability accounts and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balance of accounts.
We recommended the Cashier and the Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed time to submit these documents to COA and for the immediate supervisors to monitor its compliance.

We recommended the management to comply with and observe strictly to the provisions of Sections 50 and 51, of the Manual on the NGAS – LGUs, Volume II, requiring the monthly preparation and submission of the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer who shall render the CRAAF for submission to the Auditor’s Office through the Municipal Accountant’s Office.

The agency is recommended to prepare and submit the Annual Procurement Plan as required pursuant to provision of Section 7 of Republic Act 9184 to achieve efficiency, effectiveness and economy in the procurement of supplies and materials. Ensure that this requirement is complied with and that all purchases of goods, supplies and materials are included in or covered by the approved Annual Procurement Plan.

 All end-users of the procuring entity are recommended individually to prepare and submit an Annual Procurement Plan/PPMP to the BAC Secretariat. The Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
The Municipal Administrator should update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.
Provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.

It should be properly obligated as Accounts Payable in accordance with laws and regulations to preclude distortion of the current annual budget and possible misstatement of income.
The Municipal Accountant – designate should further effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to rectify the error, as follows:

The Municipal Accountant – designate to inform all cost center heads in advance to submit supporting documents to the Municipal Budget Officer for obligation of the expenses already incurred during the year to be charged to the current year appropriation.
The Local Chief Executive should strictly comply with the provision of Section 119 of PD 1445. Refrain from using current appropriation / allotment for payment of prior year’s obligations / transactions.
Refund the amount of P492,844.00 considering that there was no appropriation allocated for the purpose.
The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.
The Local Chief Executive to adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes.
The Municipal Accountant to analyze and determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances of some trust liability accounts and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balance of accounts.
The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the following:
All end-users of the procuring entity should individually prepare and submit the PPMP to the BAC Secretariat.
Direct the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the Annual Procurement Plan based on all the individual APP/PPMPs of the end-users, copy furnished the local auditor.
BAC thru its secretariat to prepare and submit the Annual Procurement Plan as required pursuant to provision of Section 7 of Republic Act 9184 to achieve efficiency, effectiveness and economy in the procurement of supplies and materials. Ensure that this requirement is complied with and that all purchases of goods, supplies and materials are included in or covered by the approved Annual Procurement Plan.
The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

We recommended the Supply/Property Officer to record the supplies and materials acquisitions using the perpetual inventory method as required under the New Government Accounting System (NGAS). This is done by debiting the inventory account for all receipts of supplies and materials. Any issuances of supplies and materials should be made thru a report to be submitted on a monthly basis to the accounting unit by the supplies and materials unit. In which case, the entry should be debited to supplies expense account and a credit to inventory account.
observations.managementAction: Recorded as asset, then expense upon issuance to the end user.
